feed.title: Lizstar’s Trashcan
feed.subtitle: A place where Lizstar writes about the stupid junk she wants to write about. Special interests include yuri, video games, writing, yuri, obscure video games, and a lot of yuri.

entry.title: I Wani Hug That Gator - Review and discussion

link.title: I Wani Hug That Gator - Review and discussion

entry.content:
I first discovered this game because of porn.

That’s prolly a wild way to start a review, but I should prolly make that blunt. I am a huge scalie, and I like gator girls. However, I am also quite gay, and while I really love visual novels, I tend to avoid hetero ones. But this looked cute and had very good reviews, so why not, I’ll handle the straights’ nonsense for the day.

I ended up staying up until like 4AM, and then took the day off today to finish it. I enjoyed it, quite a lot!

And then I sat down to write the review, and I did some research into the game so I could provide even the BAREST amount of background information, and oh my fucking god. I got got, y’all.

IWHTG is made by a team called Cavemanon. You may have heard of that team, you might not have. I had not. I saw the game, saw the cute scalie art style, and went “oh I like that”, but holy SHIT, reader, the backstory behind this team put such a bad taste in my mouth that I went to bed sad. It made me completely recontextualize EVERYTHING I had thought while playing this game in a way I haven’t had to do in a while.

So many years ago, there was a game coming out called “Goodbye Volcano High”. I had never heard of it. It looks kinda cute, and you MAY notice there’s a visual similarity between the styles of Volcano High and IWHTG. See, GVH is a queer rhythm/visual novel game about dinosaurs in highschool, and apparently some anonymous 4chan users saw this, and decided to create Snoot Game. Snoot Game is a visual novel using the assetts from GVH to openly mock it and twist it into something else.

Yes, the #1 thing that got me into this game, the cute art style, is entirely stolen. Well if I get ANYTHING From this, its that I should look into playing Goodbye Volcano High.

After THAT success, this team went on to make IWHTG and some other game I haven’t looked into much. Apparently they’ve also ripped people off, there’s rumors that some orders for something aren’t fulfilled, and other rumors that some people haven’t been paid for things. So all in all, Pretty Rough. You may be wondering how I didn’t realize I was playing a far right 4chan-lead project. Well, IWHTG really doesn’t give anything away itself. It’s MUCH more obvious in Snoot Game, where your main character is a faceless self insert called Anon, and it tackles heavy topics with all the grace that someone from 4chan would give them. Oh, and apparently the good ending ends with one of the nonbinary protagonists detransitioning. Apparently they die in all the other endings or something like that?

It’s blunt. IWHTG is not really like that.

In IWHtG, you play as Inco, a Human who’s recently moved to a new school for his senior year. If you look at him, he’s obviously just the typical 4chan anon self insert character all of these kinda things use, except he’s wearing sunglasses. Those characters, as I said, are self inserts with no real character or personality. Inco isn’t really like that. He seems like it immediately, but by the end, I felt it was a deconstruction.

Inco has moved around all his life from one place to another. His parents are rich, but they’re never home, and he’s basically been raised by screens. As such he doesn’t know really how to interract with others and has never been able to really make deep connections with others. I mean that DOES sound like 4chan losers, minus the rich parents prolly, but I still don’t think he’s there for you to self insert into. He is his own person. And he has a LOT of personality despite being so nondescript. He’s just… a little broken, from his kind of fucked up upbringing. Like I said, I THOUGHT it was a rather well done deconstruction.

But again, learning about their previous game and their unrepentant behavior, I wonder if I just read too much into it.

I should note, this game has four endings. I got the golden ending, and looked up descriptions for the other endings. Some of them are… kind of dark! In this game you’re kind of graded on how much Inco cares about others VS himself, and that determines endings. In hindsight, maybe this should have made me double take. One of the reviews I read, which again were all very positive because this game has suffered reverse review bombing from 4chan, mentioned “The Simp Ending”. I did not get this ending, or any other ending but the golden ending, so I assumed that wasn’t fully in proper context, but no, this game probably DOES have a simp ending, and that’s one of the bad ones.

Unrestrained simping, yes, not great, but I think it’s not great for a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT REASON these people think its not great.

Anyways Inco is at this new school and wants to make friends for the first REAL time in his life. It’s an art focused school, and Inco is in fact, really into art and culture. Specifically photography, which is neat. He’s also the only Human at the school, as he’s moved to a very Dinosaur populated area. Yes, this world has Dinosaurs as people, it’s never really explained. There’s a history class which Inco mostly ignored cause its boring, but he occasionally shows hints of this world’s past, and it’s mostly a piss take mix of our world history and whatever they want. Fair enough. Anyways, one of these people he meets at school is this.

This is Olivia, the school mess. She’s actually NOT a gator, ftr, because these are all dinosaurs, remember. So she’s a Baryonyx. Which is basically an ancient alligator. She’s a grumpy fuckup who does NOT like other people, but Inco becomes friends with her only real friend who is trying REALLY HARD to get her to open her shell, so she has to put up with Inco suddenly slamming into her life.

It is also important to note, Olivia is paraplegic. She has to use a wheelchair to get around anywhere. This is something that could be handled very, VERY poorly. Of course, I’m not disabled in that way. Just in other ways, which the game DOES comment on, and I’ll get to :) but it SEEMED fine? She beats herself up about her disability, and it’s been used to justify giving her a free ride in the past, which she hates, so she’s self destructive because she feels she doesn’t deserve it. This SEEMS fine, but considering the other things in the game, I’m side eying it just a little bit. It reminded me a little of ANOTHER 4chan visual novel dating sim, Katawa Shoujo, which I played years ago when it came out, and I will say, it’s better than that was.

So the story is basically about her and Inco slowly becoming friends, and of course more, while they both learn to grow and get past the things holding them back. Inco by making true friends, and Olivia by working past her trauma and past experiences. And of course, some current experiences too. There’s a bully who treats Olivia like shit, and eventually blackmail enters the picture. Notable, this bully says the r-word twice. At the time I was like “well that sucks, wish they hadn’t used it, but she’s an evil bully, so” but now I think they just that word is okay to use :/

There are a couple other moments in the game that made me double take, but give it the benefit of the doubt, and I’m honestly not sure what to think now. Near the end, in a prom scene, Olivia stands using her tail so that the two of you can dance. It evokes the “SHE’S CURED NOW” happy ending of some stories that are kinda shitty about disabled people, and the game ends up commenting on this, where she’s like “I can stand for a few minutes like this, even though it hurts. And I HATE that, cause now I feel guilty, like other disabled people don’t have that ability. Is that fucked up or what?” so even that, it feels like the writers thought about it, at the VERY least.

Another thing is how this game handles mental health. One of the characters has to take anti-psychotics. He’s got a LOT of built up anguish about this, and is scared about how people will treat him if they know. As someone with a lot of mental health problems, I DO get that. And he has some built up emotions about how Olivia has to live with it openly, and he’s almost jealous, cause she doesn’t have to hide it like he does. He ALSO acknowledges that’s dumb as fuck and he needs to get over it, which he does. This is, for the most part, handled pretty well.

Another thing in the game. There’s a teacher who’s really good for both Inco and Olivia, teaching them and trying to guide them to get past their inner demons. He’s pretty cool and nice, and at the end of the game, he passes away, and it ushers in the final act, where Olivia either gets past her grief in a healthy, or an unhealthy way. This was especially effective on me, because something similar happened to me in college, where my favorite teacher, who had promised to help me out with getting into grad school suddenly passed away with no warning.

This just shows you, even terrible people can create stories that really connect with you emotionally.

There are good elements to this game. A lot of good elements. Most of it is actually very, very good! I like most of the characters, I love the art style, and the romance was really cute! But learning about that backstory of the devs, it’s really hard to get past that, and it puts a bad taste in my mouth. “Just seperate the artist from the art”, you might say, but I can’t do that, and I don’t want to. I don’t want the worst people benefiting from my support. So yeah, don’t get this.

Someone HAS to have made some Scalie Girl yuri for me to consume, right? Anyone got any good recommendations?

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Discovery Season 2 episodes 1-4
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know literally nothing about Discovery, except for The Burn, and that it leads into Strange New Worlds. So uh… I’ve been dreading this one. But hey, who knows! Maybe it’ll surprise me!!

I am going to become the joker :)

Brother - ENT 7.5/10

We get a flashback of Michael first meeting Sarek, and also SPOCK! :O SPOCK, WE KNOW HIM! Discovery is now doing this thing where any time we see anything that is TOS related they do the fanfare. Neli calls it “The Starwars Episode 9 Thing” which god, she’s so right.

This is our favorite background character now. We call him “Christmas Ornament Guy”. He’s also like, the second half of Daft Punk, the first half is on the bridge.

Saru says he can smell Michael’s endrochrin system going into overdrive. Don’t do that, that’s creepy.

HE’S HERE! This is Pike, visiting the Discovery. Why? He makes it very clear, saying “look, it’s best to just be clear, out of respect to all of you. I’m being assigned as your new captain.” The reason is apparently extremely tense. There’s some “seven signals” that they’ve picked up recently.

Pike looks so different to me. Damn, two years between this and SNW, but it really is jarring how much 2 years and a different hair cut works. Anyways uh, Pike is, in fact, my favorite Starship captain. He’s AMAZING in SNW seasons 1 and 2. How is he in this? He makes a joke with Tilly, respect her, accepts she made an oopsie and put his data up for the whole bridge crew to see, went “Nah it’s fine. Everyone sit and look at this, cause it’s important you know who I am”, says he’s NOT Lorca, will never hurt or betray them, and takes time to know who everyone on the bridge is, which is great because they were all here in season 1 and I NEVER LEARNED WHO THEY ARE.

GOD I LOVE YOU, CHRISTOPHER PIKE.

If ANYTHING saves Discovery for me, it’s Pike.

Meanwhile, in the tragic sad gay room, Stamets is rewatching letters and logs from his dead boyfriend. It’s a little jarring lmfao. He announces to Tilly that he’s being transferred to a cushy retirement job. That’s a good thing! Maybe you shouldn’t work in the factory where your boyfriend was murdered. Tilly is being pushy and wants him to stay. Girl, no. Let a man rest.

We also have some stuff with Sarek being a shitty dad and Michael and Spock’s relationship being strained. This is not as interested to me as Stamets and Pike.

Everyone on the bridge crew is getting lines. They literally had no lines last season. Holy hell. The terrible AWFUL choice to barely treat these people like season 1 makes it hit even harder in season 2 that Pike is an actually GOOD captain, good person, and most of all, is STARFLEET. They turned their weakness into a strength, 100%.

They find a Federation ship down in an asteroid field, possibly with someone inside. Michael, Pike, and his two random people he came on board with head off to rescue them in some weird hamster ball ships. The science officer is a piece of shit who ignores all of Michael’s suggestions and immediately dies. What a surprise. His thing exploding almost kills Pike and makes his shuttle break.

Michael risks her life to save Pike, which works out. They go into the broken Federation ship and finds…

This is Commander Reno, she’s been stranded here for ten months. Her only fellow survivors are hooked up to life support to keep them alive. They set up a thing to beam them all out of there, but then Michael gets left behind cause of a problem happening as the ship falls apart around her.

She is gravely injured, and then sees some weird angel thing, before being rescued by Pike. Then she goes “NO!”, apparently having found some blue glowey rock, which falls through her and doesn’t beam with them. What? She was holding it?!

Apparently it’s some weird special glowing rock. It allows them to have infinite free energy. And it gave Michael visions as she was holding it.

THEY FOUND A FUCKING MARKER?!

Their treknobabble about this is hard to follow but that doesn’t seem to be important right now. They leave.

Pike’s staying on, as a “joint captain” according to him, because Enterprise is being repaired. Though before he fully takes over, Michael asks him if she can see Spock. Spock isn’t here, though… he’s taken leave. Apparently he’s been Weird lately. The Enterprise was on a deep space 5 year mission, they missed the war, and Spock has been acting ESPECIALLY weird about it.

Michael goes over to the Enterprise and listens to one of Spock’s personal logs, where he talks about having nightmares, and is gonna head out and probably die. Burnham has so much she wants to say to Spock after their shakey childhood, and prays she can find him in time.

This was mostly good!! Holy shit!

New Eden - ENT 7/10

Burnham asks Pike to help her find Spock. He explains he’s actually in a psych ward, and asked not to have anyone told. But since apparently he has these seven signals in his notes, it overrides that. But Burnham is afraid to try and reach out, since he wants nothing to do with her. Pike then is like “if you have ANYTHING to tell me, I’ll do anything I can”. She ALMOST tells him she saw an angel on that asteroid but then is like “nah”. Come on, girl.

A new signal appears and they need to jump through the mushrooms again to get to it, cause it’s 150 years away at top speed. Stamets comes out of retirement to do it, but is worried he’ll see his dead husband. After the jump he, cryingly, leaves.

As they reach this new planet, they find a planet with Humans on it. There’s a distress signal coming from it going “OH GOD SAVE US WE’RE UNDER ATTACK”, but signs on the surface are pleasant and fine. That signal has been on loop for 200 years, before warp was even discovered. Ooh, fun mystery.

There’s some weird stuff about religion. Pike what the fuck are you talking about? “This could have been an act of God” what? Did I dimensional shift into a completely different show?

Burnham, Pike, and one of the bridge crew (I haven’t learned their names yet, I am not Pike) beam down to the planet and pretend to fit in. They enter a church, which apparently hobbles together religions from Earth into a new pseudo religion, and htey also apparently worship… Digimon?

The colonists directly explain that they were rescued in World War III by an angel who took them to this new planet, Terralysium, and they built New Eden and solved their religious diversity by combining faiths. That’s uh… how that’d work.

Back on the Discovery, there’s some thing about Tilly almost dying to find some way to stop Stamets from needing to do mushroom magic. Also they find out some space thing is going to make the planet unlivable in 60 minutes. Saru hypothesizes that’s why the signal was here. To rescue the colonists.

Back in New Eden, Pike refuses to take these people back, because of “General Order One”, which is their excuse for getting around the Prime Directive not being here yet. They are kidnapped by a crazy guy who believes they’re from Earth and followed his distress beacon his family has kept here forever. Wow, what a wild conspiracy theory.

Tilly theorizes from Sick Bay a way to save everyone, with the help of some cute nurse who is weirdly flirty with her. There’s no way this girl is weird, she’s some weird angel messenger. Ah well, Gay Tilly will have to be but a dream.

Pike sacrifices himself to save a child who’s messing with their guns, and is dying. They use the church as a smokescreen to beam back, pretending that the angels saved them.

Tilly looks up who this nurse was, May Ahearn, an old friend of her’s apparently. The computer is like “They are not on this ship :) They are dead.” Welp. Spooky ghost!

Michael comes clean to Pike about what happened to her on the asteroid. She thought she was hallucinating, but seeing the art of the Angels on New Eden, it’s clear. She saw one of those Angels. Something is up.

They go back down to the crackpot in New Eden, after Michael convinces Pike. He has a soldier’s old helmet camera that recorded the Angels. They trade the helmet for a power cell that’ll help power his trinkets he’s been working on, and tell him the truth. He thanks then, and lights up the church. And then assumedly afterwards, the other colonists burn him at the stake.

Point of Light - ENT 5/10

The “last time on” is all about Ash Tyler. God no, don’t bring him back. Leave all that in the past, we were doing so well.

Tilly is still seeing this old childhood friend, “May”. She’s very friendly and CLEARLY a figment of her mind, and she’s having a nervous breakdown over it. Poor Tilly.

Meanwhile, Sarek’s ship shows, and Amanda beams over, needing Michael’s help.

I guess we need to do more Klingon stuff. Okay. The Klingons are unhappy at the new chancellor trying to unite them cause they’re old assholes who still want power, and also I guess she has a human torchbearer. I mean, everyone KNOWS he’s actually Klingon but y’know, they’re weird about it.

It’s REALLY weird to watch Star Trek and go “can we get LESS Klingon stuff”. This ends with him realizing Voq has a son, and making a plea that he’ll be his father.

Anyways back to the GOOD plot, turns out Spock is wanted for murder. Three of the doctors are dead and he fled the starbase. Also we get a great Pike quote. “I could break into this file but that’d be breaking the rules, and my mother wouldn’t like that.” God, he’s such a boyscout.

Tilly has a nervous breakdown and screams at her apparition in the head. To everyone ELSE, she screams at the captain, then quits on the spot.

POV: you just said something racist on the bridge. Anyways you’d think this’d be red flag for “has an alien in her head”.

Oh my GOD, Amanda mentioned Logic Extremists again. PLEASE do not let that come back. Amanda and Michael have a long conversation about how Spock hates her, and then Amanda leaves in anger. What? I feel like I missed something in that scene.

Tilly FINALLY tells someone what’s going on, Michael, who immediately realizes it’s Spore related and tells her to go talk to Stamets. Damn, thank you Michael. Wish that was solved earlier.

We get a big fight scene with Klingons, because some enemies to the throne kidnapped their baby. Okay.

What the FUCK is this Star Wars shit

Okay, it’s Georgiou, who is now a “security consultant”. And is apparently here to make sure they stay on the throne. She’s also here to force an ultimatum. Kill the baby, and Tyler. She does, and then becomes a Klingon cult leader, basically. They call her “mother”. Is it GOOD when your dictator wants you to call them mother? Anyways, actually the baby and Tyler leave, cloaked in a Federation ship. Oh boy, a war crime! Hey, it’s Section 31 :) Fucking lovely. Remember when that was just something one guy and a couple weirdos were doing in DS9? I miss that. Anyways Tyler joins Section 31. I hate that.

They determine it’s an intelligent fungal entity that has invaded Tilly’s mind, and immediately suck it out of her. Uh, that’s an intelligent creature, maybe we should first contact- okay.

This one was not as good.

An Obol For Charon - ENT 9/10

What the fuck is this image

We start with… Una arriving on Discovery! Hello, Number One! :D Good to see her.

They really went with the retro futurism look with her here. She’s hot af like this tbh, I like that kinda style.

There’s a Saurian on staff. We’ve never really seen him before. He’s very casual and pleasant. His name is Linus. I would die for Linus.

While she’s asking Pike to save Spock, they are pulled out of warp by a giant angry red ball. Okay. While studying it, suddenly everyone starts speaking random languages. Oh boy, it’s the Orb Of Babel. Luckily, Saru knows a bunch of languages. He goes around, saving the day, cleaning out a virus in the universal translator. He also has a cold. Poor Saru.

This is Jett Reno. She was on that asteroid a few episodes back, seems she’s still here. Thank god, I REALLY like her. She’s EXTREMELY casual, and a tiny bit rude, and I legit love her. She gets in a pissing match with Stamets about engineering and dilithium being better than fungus, but then they start working together. But during problems, power goes out and the fungus gets out, connecting to Tilly like a fucking Symbiote.

Holy shit, they’re gonna drill a hole in Tilly’s head. I’ve had nightmares about this. OKAY IT WAS EVEN MORE HORRIFYING TO WATCH THEM DO IT.

Saru is not just sick. He thinks the sphere is triggering the Kelpien biological thing called Vahar’ai. Kelpiens going through Vahar’ai either go mad, or are culled. Either way, he’s dead. And he’s given up, he just wants to save the ship, as whatever’s happening with this orb is clearly causing danger. He also thinks he can communicate with it.

They think the Sphere is dying and wants to be remembered so it’s trying to transmit what it knows to them.

It’s weird that we’re ALSO doing stuff with this fungus at the same time, cause it’s NOT related to the sphere. At all. It’s very distracting. Both A and B plots here are good but they’re not related in any way. It explains its a “jahSepp”, here to talk to Stamets. Stamets has been causing problems with his jumps. Also it has “plans” for Tilly, and is kind of… ominous and evil. When explaining it refuses to let go of Tilly, Reno pulls out a fucking blowtorch and is like “Not on my watch”. Holy SHIT she is cool as hell. They break Tilly out, the end?

The Sphere sends its message, then pushes them out of the way and dies. Saru announces that it’s now HIS turn to die. The bridge crew stands to honor him as he leaves, hugging his friend, Burnham, who escorts him to his room.

This is actually very emotional and well done. I’ve never been into Burnham that much, and her and Saru have had a weird relationship where they were beleagured colleagues, then very hesitant colleagues, now they’re best friends. While it doesn’t feel like it’s been super well built up, it feels very good here. I can’t hold the misfortunes of season 1 against it.

Saru asks Michael to sever his ganglia, which are causing him SEVERE pain, and kill him. She can barely do it. It’s very touching. Anyways before Michael can even touch them, they fall out on their own and his vital signs return to normal. Whoop, he’s not dying!

His threat ganglia are gone, and now they are no longer governing him. He isn’t constantly feeling fear. He feels a new sense of power, but it is troubling in a way. His people revere this, and how it kills them. It’s their truth. But uh, it doesn’t kill them! It’s a lie! He’s suffering from a mild shock about it, but realizes that it could be far FAR worse for his people. This whole thing also made Burnham realize she needs to stick with Spock no matter what.

The jahSepp makes them really high and then eats Tilly and teleports her somewhere somehow. To be continued?

I was wildly interested in this, this is by FAR the best Discovery has been.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Short Treks 1-4
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

While Disco was being made, they made Short Treks basically at the same time. We should watch those too I guess. This is a Disco side series about the Disco characters. Cool.

Runaway - ENT 4/10

Some weird alien gets on board, and Tilly is eating food alone in the mess hall. I know she’s alone cause they made these in a day with only a few people working on them but it’s just very funny that the mess hall is 100% empty.

After talking to her frankly very Lwaxana Troi coded mom, Tilly meets the alien, who makes a mess. It hurt itself on something, cutting and bleeding everywhere. It can cloak itself somehow and kinda turns itself humanoid, but is chittering all over the place. It actually seems to be a she. She likes ice cream! She is a “Xaheean” named Po.

Tilly has to hide Po like she’s a cat that she’s trying to keep, and not a sapient creature that people would treat with support, because they’re Federation?

There’s some weird thing about the Xaheean viewing their planet as their twins, I don’t really get it. Also some nonsense about her creating a “Dilithium Resequencer” or something. My brain is turning off.

She reveals she’s actually the princess and is gonna have her coronation tomorrow. She gives a special dilithium crystal to Tilly as a gift. They’re already setting up Dilithium as this special macguffin, huh? Not knowing wtf it is? Yeah.

A LITTLE closer to the vibes of Trek but still with a stink of Discovery nonsense.

Calypso - ENT 6.5/10

This episode is, immediately, VERY flashy for some reason.

A guy in a broken ship and life support pod floats through space then wakes up in the Discovery. He’s all alone, and it’s dark. He assumes he’s captured and tries breaking out, but is contacted by an AI who calls herself Zora. The guy’s name is Craft, he’s a Human soldier who’s only known war.

This episode is shot in a really creepy way. It’s like a psychological horror. The low lighting of Disco and the emptiness of Short Trek really fits here.

This appears to be a far, far time in the future. The Discovery is empty, and abandoned for years. Zora does her best to make Craft feel comfortable. It’s actually NOT creepy or scary, but it’s weirdly shot that way at times. Craft wants to do something nice for her.

He helps her get a holographic body, and they dance. They ALMOST kiss, but Craft remembers his original family and leaves. She’s like “wait, but I’m not a person, it doesn’t mean anything”, which is pretty sad. Realizing she can’t keep him, she helps him leave, sending him off home, and then playing the dance to herself over and over.

Mild spoilers but this episode will be KIND of retconned. It takes place in the 43rd century now, which is the furthest in the timeline we ever see. Neat!

The Brightest Star - ENT 8/10

It’s a Saru episode! He’s talking about his childhood. His people are prey animals, remember. So he’s talking about how his people kind of revere being hunted and dying to the Ba’ul, the other sapient people on the planet. Thats kinda fucked.

His father is like “Yes the Ba’ul fly with space ships. You cannot, Saru. If the great balance wanted us to fly he would have given us wings. We must keep the balance and be eaten because thats the way it always has been!” Hey look, a real life metaphor that isn’t terrible bad! It’s almost Trek-like!

Saru keeps some technology that fell even though it’s forbidden in his village. He’s a thinker, he wants to know more of the universe. So he keeps messing with it… and he gets a message back.

He says goodbye to his sister, and says he just “wants to watch the stars for a while”. And then he’s found by Georgiou. She explains that while they got his message and are here to meet and take him like he requested, he IS a part of a pre-warp society, so he won’t really be able to come back. He decides his place is no longer there, so he goes with Georgiou into the stars.

The Escape Artist - ENT 8/10

This was the first thing about Disco I had ever seen. I remember liking it, and it made me excited to see more Harry Mudd. I have never been so betrayed in my life.

Anyways Mudd is captured by a bounty hunter and given to an Tellarite who hates him cause Mudd fucked his sister and stole from him.

He’s also apparently wanted in the Federation for 30 attempts of homicide, and one attempt of regicide??? Harry Mudd is a con man and with used car salesman energy, why the fuck did they make him just straight up evil with no positive qualities whatsoever? Was he always like this, did I forget what Harry Mudd was like in TOS?

He’s also wanted for one count of “penetrating a space whale”, which at the time I thought was kinda silly, and now it makes me irrationally angry.

He spends this episode trying to worm his way out of custody. He claims he’s part of “The Resistance” against the Federation, which is very silly. The episode also keeps cutting back and forth to when he has done the same lines and been in the same situations previous times he’s been in custody.

He starts complaining about the Federation’s taxes, a thing the federation is famous for having.

In every previous encounter, he is seen fucking up and getting the shit beat out of him. Good.

And then we get the twist, as the Tellarite takes Mudd to the Federation, and they reveal that he fell for a scam. Bounty hunters have been turning up with these android fakes for a while. There’s some stuff about fake DNA skin. They even open a door and show a room full of Mudd clones.

Kinda a fun twist tbh.

We then find out the REAL Mudd was the lady alien selling Mudds to everyone. Tee hee.

If I liked this version of Mudd I think this’d be great. As it is, I’m not sure why the hell they wanted to make him chaotic neutral Han Solo, and even then they overshot that REAL far into chaotic evil han solo.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 222 - The Preservation Project

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 222 - The Preservation Project

entry.content:
I had just finished B3313, a romhack of Mario 64 designed to look and feel like a lost beta of the game. You’d THINK after 450 stars of B3313 I’d be done, but nah. I had heard about this and wanted to check it out.

The Preservation Project is a pretty overly designed name for what is just straight up a recreation of a Mario 64 beta. It’s a recreation of the Mario 64 spaceworld beta from images and video. That’s it. I gotta say, “The Preservation Project” is a sick-ass name, so I get why they called it that rather than just “Mario 64 Space World Beta Recreation”.

Is it accurate? I dunno, it sure looks pretty accurate. I was not in Kyoto in the mid 90s to check out the Spaceworld demos. It’s fun as a curiosity, at least. It takes about 20 minutes to see everything, which makes sense, it’s a demo to show stuff off, not an actual game. I always liked the vibes of early demo images. It’s weirdly empty and nostalgic in a way I can’t quite place. But I like lookin’ at em.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 223 - Mr. Do’s Wild Ride

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 223 - Mr. Do’s Wild Ride

entry.content:
A new Dev Jam came and went. And with it, a new console that a bunch of devs made a bunch of games for. This time it was the MSX, that classic Japanese computer with a bunch of games most Americans have never played or even heard of, but was absolutely HUGE in Japan. It’s an extremely important ecosystem, just like the PC88-98 is, but is largely ignored in the west. I first learned about the MSX from La-Mulana!

But despite that, not every MSX game is a classic that everyone remembers, OR some strange Japanese novelty. A lot of them are just arcade ports. Like this. Fun fact btw, this was dev’d by me. I did this :)

Mr. Do’s Wild Ride is an arcade game from the early 80s about everyone’s second favorite clown, Mr. Do. He was pretty big back then, somehow, and he got a lot of games for someone who had a B-tier digdug clone. My favorite is Do Run Run, but my second favorite is this, Mr. Do’s Wild Ride. It’s nothing like any of the other games. You play as Mr. Do running from one end of a rollercoaster to another for some fucking reason. You need to time your movements and climbing in time with the cars flying past you.

If you get hit, Mr. Do goes flying wildly across the screen in a fun little jump scare. You can also collect cherries for bonus points. If you hold down the button, you use up your time to run extra fast, which can be good for getting past scary timings.

This version is okay. It’s not nearly as good as the arcade version, but come on, it’s the MSX. It’s missing a level, but it’s missing my least favorite level so that’s fine. It is what it is. There are worse arcade conversions out there, but it’s not like anyone would go back to this, except some fucking weirdo. Oh wait, I’m that weirdo.

entry.title: LizstarTrek - Lower Decks Season 1
entry.content:
Every Sunday my Twitch subs and I watch Star Trek in my Discord. Here are my reviews and thoughts on each of the episodes as I see it. If you’re curious about a nerd’s views on Star Trek episodes, please read on, I go quite in depth with some of these episodes! I’ve watched some Star Trek before but not all of it!

I know very little about this show except it was made by nerds like me with a very different sense of humor. God, please be good.

Second Contact - ENT 3.5/10

We’re introduced very quickly to two Ensigns. Boimler and Mariner. Boimler is high strung, lame, and has illusions of grandeur, making a fake captains log. Mariner is a kinda shitty killjoy who has EXTREME illusions of grandeur. Mariner injurs Boimler with a batleth while drunk on Romulan Ale. Okay, next scene!

We’re now introduced to Ensign Tendi, an Orion who’s very bright eyed and bushy tailed, but y’know, she’s lower decks so she’ll be getting beat down over time I’m sure. They also meet another ensign, Rutherford, an engineer who’s a cyborg and ALSO a nervous wreck. There’s a lot of that here. Hey, is this review fast and hard to follow without a lot of detail? Yes! That’s HOW THIS SHOW IS INTRODUCED.

Everyone is talking so fucking fast, jokes are flung in your face every 5 seconds with no time to breath and then we move right on to the next joke or the next scene. If you speak REALLY fast everyone will think what you say is smart, except for Funny this time. It is… NOT easy to follow. And the jokes are pretty rough, IIRC. Lots of awkward humor, which is VERY not my speed.

Boimler is told by her captain to keep an eye on Mariner, and he takes it way WAY too seriously and makes things really fucking weird. That’s his character trait! The two of them scare off some aliens at the planet they’re on and then get attacked by a spider that shoots webbing out of its butt. You might think that’s weird for me to specify, but the show sure does! Ha ha, butts are funny!

Meanwhile, one of the bridge officers got infected with a zombie virus and its spreading violently through the ship. The show cuts from scenes of graphic zombie violence to Rutherford having a date. It’s incongruent. That’s the point and it’s supposed to be funny…. okay, it’s actually not bad. I kinda like this part. They’re walking around “on a date walk” as they blast zombies and talk about the music they’re into. Thats kinda cute. Unfortunately the date doesn’t work cause Rutherford is kind of a huge dork.

Why are their clothes off? Don’t worry about it.

Boimler gets covered in alien slime goo and is taken to sick bay cause it’s a cure for the virus. All of the bridge crew ignore Boimler and his contributions and they are basically having their own Star Trek episode while the Lower Deckers are in the background. I think that’s supposed to be the entire premise and conceit of the entire show but also I kinda hate it? It’s like, really unpleasant to watch.

You gotta take this show with a BIT more of a grain of salt cause it’s a hyperbolic joke show, but this is still not my favorite general conceit. And I’d LOVE a show about lower decks people.

Anyways, Boimler does NOT narc on Mariner, and they become “friends”. Mariner is also the captain’s daughter, who hates her and wants to throw her in the brig for no reason.

This was so fucking hard to follow and write a review of holy HELL. This was a very rough introduction, but I can see how there are sparks of something good here.

Envoy - ENT 4.5/10

Our episode starts with Mariner harassing some evil space wisp with super cosmic power. She bullies it into using up its power and it dies slamming into the captain. Ha ha?

Anyways this episode has an A and a B plot. The A plot is that Boimler is escorting a Klingon warrior down to a planet, while Mariner forces her way on board and ruins it cause she’s actually besties with the Klingon.

Meanwhile, Rutherford promised Tendi that he’s do a thing with her once but he keeps overloading himself on Engineering work. So he decides to change his entire career and is kinda bummed about it. Doesn’t do anything half assed, does he?

Boimler and Mariner get themselves stranded in a seedy Klingon section of a planet cause the Klingon stole their shuttle for a joke.

Rutherford is trying command simulations with the first officer, Jack Ransom. He blows up the ship and kills all the children, specifically. Jack is WEIRDLY into the idea of killing children. Is that. Supposed to be a joke? What? What is this character?

The bridge officers are, for the most part, kind of assholes. The captain and first officer especially, but there are two I kinda like so far. The doctor is a ratty mean Caitian who, while blunt, actually seems to care and understand how people work? And the security officer, who’s a giant braga braga Bajoran and seems like fun tbh. He even takes Rutherford wanting to go back to Engineering super well cause he’s just a nice guy! That’s cool.

Mariner, despite not giving a shit about anything, is instantly good at every single thing she does. It makes Boimler feel so bad that he decides to quit. Then he gets his groove back after being racist at a Ferengi saying they are the least trustworthy race in space. What? What the fuck? Quark did not die for your sins so that you could talk like that. Not to mention ROM. …Okay, the whole Ferengi thing was just a plot by Mariner to make Boimler feel better. Good on her. Bad on you, Boimler >:(

A little better.

Temporal Edict - ENT 5/10

The captain has a bug up her ass and finds out that the lower decks crew have a thing called “Buffer Time” where they do things slightly half assed by giving extra time estimates on things so they look good and don’t have to rush. She puts a tighter leash on everyone. This IMMEDIATELY backfires and everyone is super overworked. Ah yes, let’s reinvent capitalistic practices. Everyone hates it. Oh except Boimler, he’s having a good time.

Everyone being overworked and exhausted means they make mistakes and piss off some aliens they were supposed to give a gift to.

I think Captain Freeman might be my least favorite Captain so far, and that’s nuts considering one of the options is Captain Lorca.

Ransom and Mariner fight over who has to fight in a battle to the death. Ransom stabs her to get her to stop. There’s also some kind of sexual tension between the two of them I am REALLY not into.

Ha ha ethics are for losers and pencil pushers. I won’t justify that because the show doesn’t.

Anyways they win by loosening up, of course. Boimler suggests it and gets something named after him, “The Boimler Effect”, where you’re supposed to cut corners and do what you can to get out of more work. He is not happy. And far in the future, Boimler is revered for being a lazy piece of shit. But he’s not as revered as “the most important person in Starfleet History”

This is correct.

This one had two jokes I liked!!

Moist Vessel - ENT 5/10

Mariner is rude during a meeting with another captain and this pisses the captain off. She does it cause she’s yawning cause “it’s too much information to take in all at once”. I dunno if this show has the right to say that, considering how fucking fast paced it is.

Why are there class divided replicators? That’s weird.

The captain assigns Mariner to the shittiest jobs on the ship to try and force her to want to quit. Meanwhile, Tendi messes up someone’s ascension to a higher plane of existence and it’s a SERIOUS social faux pas. Yeah, I imagine.

Mariner makes her shitty jobs still somehow fun and loose and that can’t do, so they come up with a NEW plan to make her suffer. They promote her.

Now she gets to sit in on meetings about what kinda chairs we should have in the meeting room, and in on the friendly poker night :D Okay, this is an actually funny joke to me. ACTUALLY good conceit. Can’t wait to see how they fuck it up.

I don’t like the Tendi half though, it’s nothing but awkward humor. Though when she says “I HATE when people don’t like me, it’s like ants in my brain!” holy shit I feel that.

The mission they are ACTUALLY trying to do falls apart cause everyone is being a dick to each other and not paying attention to it, just trying to one up each other and focus on what THEY want not what needs to be done.

They manage to fix everything cause yeah, Mariner is actually good at her job she’s just kind of a dick. The guy Tendi’s been harassing ACTUALLY ascends. It’s horrifying. I think the people who got mutated in the transporter in the first movie suffered less.

The show makes fun of how people say Sensors in Star Trek. Has it earned that? Maybe. I kinda like the joke. Anyways Mariner is demoted again cause she makes fun of an admiral’s pronunciation of sensors, and the status quo is back.

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 3/10

Let’s play a game. Can you name every reference in this image?

The Cerritos is meeting up with some ship that everyone says is super cool and important for some reason. I dunno why but like, okay. Boimler’s girlfriend is on the ship and is visiting. Mariner is freaked out at first, but turns out, she is great! A little TOO GREAT. And we now have your stereotypical “jealous of your girlfriends’ ex” plotline which I dunno. Boimler is hyper focused on that, while Mariner is CERTAIN she’s a shape changing evil alien.

Meanwhile, we got B AND C plots. The captain is trying to convince weird conspiracy theory aliens to let htem blow up a moon that’ll kill them if they don’t. And Tendi and Rutherford are on the other starship getting jealous of how cool it is. Somehow.

There’s a joke where they talk about how great Trip Tucker was and it legit made us upset.

The “Boimler is jealous” jokes are EXACTLY what I feared them to be. Bad :) I do not like these kind of jokes or this kind of humor. Eventually they get past it.

This is an image of Boimer naked wanting to do sexy times. This episode moves too fast to write up stuff. It’s not very good or interesting anyway tbh. Turns out the aliens were just two rich eccentric assholes so they just ignore them and blow up the moon anyway, then Boimler’s girlfriend and Mariner bond over how lame and bad at everything Boimler is. Okay. Also Boimler was infected a parasite that made him irresitable. She goes out with the parasite instead.

Didn’t like this one much.

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 5.5/10

This episode introduces Fletcher, an old acadamy buddy of Boimler’s. He’s a nice guy, but is ever so slightly awkward.. He buddies up to Boimler and Mariner very quickly. Meanwhile, Rutherford and Tendi hang out in a holodeck training program.

Okay, a thing I’m noticing. Why is EVERY episode Boimler and Mariner team up, while Tendi and Rutherford team up? Can’t we like, split them up every now and then into different configurations?

Fletcher is attacked and some stuff is stolen. They don’t go to the officers cause why would they that’d be silly. He’s clearly making shit up to get out of fucking up.

Rutherford invented a training program AI called Badgey. It’s very clearly a Clippy thing, and is extremely sinister. I like it. When the ship is attacked, the holodeck loses its safety protocols and Badgey starts attacking violently. Uh, got our first Shit Goes Wrong In The Holodeck episode I guess.

Holy shit Badgey rips some heads off. Well, it is an “adult animation”, so that means it’s gotta have SOME weird gratuitous violence.

Whoops Feltcher got some brain damage and caused an evil thing to happen. Basically his personality is uploaded to a computer core. Turns out he’s kinda awful. They tie him up so he doesn’t get in the way. Fair. THey kick it out an airlock and it blows up the ship attacking them. Fletcher is promoted off the ship for his daring creativity and genius…. Ha ha??

Cupid’s Errant Arrow - ENT 6.5/10

Tendi re-created a dog from scratch. Ha ha nope it’s a horrifying spider monster. That actually made me giggle. And it’s still a cute perfect pupper :3 It turns into a cube. I want one. Tendi loves it regardless. Oh no, I AM Tendi aren’t I?

A sub captain comes to visit and take over for a while. Turns out it’s Mariner’s old best friend! Oh boy, surely this’ll be wonderful. Meanwhile Boimler wants to kiss up but he goes throuh some kind of phasic nonsense and is out of alignment. So they send him to The Farm upstate. No seriously it’s called the Farm. Okay good, this time Tendi and Boimler are together, good.

Meanwhile, Mariner goes down with the new captain on an away mission. She’s immediately incompetent and bad at everything and the rest of the captain’s crew doesn’t like her. What? She’s always been hyper competent that’s like her character trait. I think the idea is she FEELS inadequet next to her best friend who is now a captain but it doesn’t come through super well?

Turns out the Farm is where they send freaks they can’t fix so they don’t ruin the shiny prestige of Starfleet. They even have one of the fish lizards from the end of Threshold. The “freaks” as they call themselves plan a mutiny. The dude in charge finds out and charges to kill them all cause they have no rights. What the fuck is going on? Like yes it’s a joke and the series is hyperbolic about everything but this is like the most dark thing I’ve ever seen and they’re playing it VERY straight.

Okay turns out she’s INTENTIONALLY fucking up. She’s just a VERY good actor. She’s fucking up cause she doesn’t want to be forced into a command job. She just wants to be “a super good ensign”.

Okay turns out the farm is real everyone just completely misunderstood the situation cause the guy in charge has a short leash and is SUPER creepy and menacing. He says he’s working on it. I also like that he’s one of the three armed guys from TAS. Boimler loses his phasy-ness and is kicked out for not being a freak.

Mariner and the captain end on good terms. Mariner says she still has stuff to figure out. In the end I kinda liked this one? Has some weird problems, but in the end it twisted itself to some funny ends.

Veritas - ENT 4/10

The Lower Deckers are put in front of an alien tribunal to give testimony for the actions of the bridge crew. They demand that Mariner explains what happened on a certain stardate. In that date, they were on the bridge as some aliens were attacking over a misunderstanding about a map of the neutral zone. The tribunal doesn’t like this and asks Rutherford about another unrelated date.

It’s a bunch of weird unrelated plots. Basically any time Rutherford’s implant updates he passes out and it takes over, being super competent. But he blacks out a lot. So it jumps from scene to scene. Including a weird Gorn Wedding scene.

Tendi is super sus. She clearly knows something.

Yeah so basically she was accidentally roped into a commando bravo mission with Ransom. They’re all very Broohaha marine style about all this and going into the neutral zone for stupid nonsense.

She gets too into it and beats the shit out of some armed guards. The tribunal doesn’t believe her.

Boimler defends his bosses by saying yes they fuck up cause no one actually ever knows what they’re doing, and they’re NEVER told what’s happening ever. But they do their best. Turns out this guy holding them in trial, Clar, actually is just throwing a party for celebration of them. It’s weird.

I’m not sure about this one. Also it turned Q into Myzlplx for some reason.

Crisis Point - ENT 2/10

Mariner frees a species of lizard people from rat tyrany. Captain Freeman tells her off cause Prime Directive. Oh please do not tackle that topic you clearly can’t handle it. She gets sent off for therapy, cause it’s… bad to want to stop this? What the fuck is this show trying to say

No seriously, that’s a big problem. I’ve been wondering it quietly but Neli broached the subject. What is it TRYING to say? Starfleet bad? Starfleet good? It never seems to know.

Boimler makes a holodeck tool using the crew’s private logs so he can practice brown nosing them. Mariner hijacks it to turn it into a movie so she can let out frustrations. Boimler continues to try and brown nose. It’s awkward and embarassing. It’s filled with a lot of jokes about the way the TNG movies were filmed. It’s scarily accurate. But I didn’t LIKE those movies so…

Credit to all the lens flares and dimmer lighting though.

In the movie, Mariner plays a space warlord trying to take over the ship. Rutherford goes off to geek out with the Hologram Lead Engineer, while Tendi is like “what the fuck this is gross”. Mariner is ALSO racist about Orions, and Tendi eventually just walks out.

The ship crashes pretty much exactly like in Generations.

LOL okay good joke. “It’s okay sir I did a repeating beaming sequence and saved them all” “That’s literally impossible??” “No, it’s a movie, you can do whatever you want in a movie.” “Oh, okay!”

Mariner’s simulated self fights her real angry self.

What THE FUCK, THIS SHOW JUST DROPPED THE F SLUR. Rutherford went off with his boss after they confess their love for each other, and the doctor calls them the f-word. This is not a joke. Like, not in a bad way? But like…

no. You did not earn that. What in the fuck.

Anyways it all ends, Mariner feels better about it, says therapy works, and all’s good. Also Boimler finds out Mariner is the captain’s daughter, which is apparently a secret.

The memory alpha page says it says “fucks”. It’s bleeped. But it DEFINETLY sounds like ts at the end, not cks, and the lip flaps fit the f slur.

Okay, I found an uncensored version of the scene. She says “Godspeed, you crazy fucks” for sure. Holy SHIT it did not sound like that. And the context did not help. Okay. It’s fine then.

Anyways I still didn’t like this much. It just seemed kind of unhinged in a way I didn’t appreciate, cause it turns out her murdering everyone was good therapy and Tendi was wrong! What?

No Small Parts - ENT 3.5/10

This episode starts with them calling the TOS era the TOS era. It stands for Those Old Scientists. Alright. They just stopped Landru, from The Return of the Archons, from doing that thing he does. I don’t even think I remember that episode.

Anyways Boimler tells Mariner he knows her mom is her mom. Right as her mom is watching them and broadcasting them to the bridge. So now EVERYONE knows. Whoops!

:o there’s a new Exocomp on the crew. She’s a genki girl. I love her already. Her name is Peanut Hamper. Tendi is being weird about trying to make sure she has help. Is this a take on people with disabilities?

Everyone is suddenly EXTREMELY weird about Mariner. They’re giving her gifts and treating her extremely differently, so she wants to dip as soon as possible. But there’s no way she can transfer like this, so she decides to take things seriously and be a perfect little ensign for a while so she can get off the ship.

They beam in to help someone with what they think is routine, find the ship completely destroyed by some scary ship, and then nearly destroyed themselves. Who could this mystery villain be?? It’s… it’s… the Pacleds.

Yes.

THe fucking Pacleds. Remember them? They’re the ones who were a joke. Every alien is a “species of hats” and their hat is that they are supposed to be R-words. It was the 80s. They were a massive mistake. Anyways they’re here now, but they’re EXTREMELY menacing and evil instead. They were always morally bankrupt (ha ha, cause people with mental disabilities are actually evil). That whole thing is toned down, it’s not really here. But I can’t help but fucking think about it every time they’re back.

They literally go “wait but you’re a joke”. So it’s entirely on purpose.

They need Peanut Hamper to save the day. She says no, she’s not gonna sacrifice her life. I mean, fair, but she’s kinda a dick about it. She sayus she was just here to piss off her dad, and just dips.

Rutherford goes instead, with Shaxs the security officer, and uses a virus made by Badgey to blow them up. Badgey betrays them and Shaxs sacrifices himself to save Rutherford, dying in the process.

More Pacleds show up, but are scared off by… THE TITAN! Commanded by William Riker (who Mariner is friends with btw), who is cool and scares them off easily.

Rutherford lost his memory, but Tendi takes this as a time to become best friends all over again. Okay. I guess that’s a fun subversion of this thing. The captain makes Mariner her personal “you can do illegal things when I need them”. Her personal Section 31? That’s kinda fucked up. And Boimler uses all this to sneak a promotion over to the Titan, and Mariner is PISSED. The end.

I’m not sure I liked this season very much. But I’m not gonna write off this series yet, I hear it gets better. Here’s hopin’!

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 221 - B3313
entry.content:
This is a big one. This is actually currently my biggest set I’ve mastered, and it’s not even close. The FF4 Four Job Fiesta set, which involves you beating the game three times, is 167 achievements. This is 298.

It is a BIG ONE.

It’s almost doing this game a disservice to review it. I could come on here and give you the True Lore Version. This is a lost copy of Mario 64 found at a garage sale, filled with unknown cursed evils, dive deep. In reality, it’s a romhack based on Mario 64 betas and creepy pastas. Like, obviously. It’s not ACTUALLY a cursed cartridge. But it’s trying to intentionally give that kind of energy, and it succeeds EXTREMELY well at that.

It starts off very simply. You start in a recreation of the Spaceworld demo that, for the longest time, we only had screenshots of. It’s a very nice recreation. You even have the weird beta sound effects that are just old loony toons screams and sped up Michael Jackson for some reason. But then as you collect stars, things… shift. The game constantly changes and shifts in ways that are almost imperceptible. The numbers on doors change, for seemingly no reason. Transitions are non-euclidian. You get a number of stars and now things change even MORE, and MORE, until by the end, your map looks like fucking nonsense, and you have over 400 stars. Yes, there are 450 stars in this hack. It’s a VERY large hack.

If you’re curious, here’s my map I made of this hack. Spoilers, obviously. I’m told this is the most readable map some people have seen. Most of them look like this!!

The most funp art of making your own map is you get to name each hub area based on what YOU think the most important landmarks are. I had lots like “beta lobby”, “vanilla upstairs”, but then I have ones like “Toad Long Hall”, “Slow Giant Castle”, “Red Fog Lobby”, and “Nazca Line Basement”.

There are a LOT of levels and areas to explore in B3313, and while not all of them are bangers, and some are actively annoying (looking at you, Bowser’s Factory), the game is genuinely something impressively magical, and I am straight up sad I will never be able to play it blind again.

There’s just a vibe coming off of B3313. It tastes like nostalgia that you’ve experienced and loved, but shifted, twisted. Changed by your memory, just ever so slightly off where you can convince yourself you dreamed that as a kid, or you had just forgotten. And I fucking love that feeling. I seriously can’t recommend it enough.

Oh, it IS important to note, it does have a few jump scares. If that’s a thing you dislike. There’s not many and they’re PRETTY obvious that they’ll be coming up.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 220 - Ripple Island
entry.content:
In many ways, Ripple Island genuinely stuns me. It is one of the best looking Famicom games and is massively ahead of its time. In other ways, it’s massively OF its time. This is a 1988 Sunsoft visual novel. Sunsoft was really coming into their own around this time on the Famicom, and would make some of the very best action games. They’re well known for Blaster Master, Batman, Journey to Silius, Gimmick… not so much the visual novels. This only came out in Japan, and we sadly didn’t get it over here.

In Ripple Island you play as Kyle, a young boy who lives on the island. The evil Emperor Groaker uses magic to ravage the island and kidnap princess Nasarell. The king declares that whoever shall stop the fiend will get Nasarell’s hand in marriage, so Kyle sets off to save his home. Along the way you travel through a beautiful and varied island, talk to a bunch of cute bunnies, and team up with a girl named Cal.

A LOT of Japanese visual novels of the time had the same gameplay loop. You had to talk to every single character, exhaust all dialogue trees, look at everything, touch everything, then you could move on, Ripple Island is NOT like that. It’s a lot more like a classic Western point and click, than anything. You explore a small confined area, grab items, use them against other items or objects in the scenery, solve simple minor puzzles, and then move on.

Ripple Island takes place over five chapters, which are their own contained little areas. This helps keep it segmented and doesn’t let it get super convoluted or bloated. The areas are nice, varied, and have some good puzzles.

To really enjoy Ripple Island, you’d need to like that classic adventure style of game. As the world’s biggest Zork fan, I do, in fact, like that style of game. But it’s not for everyone. Despite that, I think its charm really shines through. It’s not very long, and rather easy for this style of adventure game, so I do highly recommend it.

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 219 - Niñoid
entry.content:
Another day, another tiny homebrew that’s basically just a demo. This time it’s Niñoid, by Three Twins Games. It’s a metroidvania I decided to play while still kind of in a Metroid mood. I played it right after Junkoid, which I didn’t master, but might talk about someday.

Anyways, Niñoid wears its inspirations on its sleeve, as you can see from this screenshot. You play as a kid chasing after his cat. You go into some ancient cat temple and get cat themed upgrades to proceed. There are only like ten rooms. Like I said, it’s more of a demo than anything else. You can beat the game in under 2 minutes. But for whats here, it’s cool? I wish the devs would do more with this engine!

entry.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 217 - Final Fantasy 1 - Economic Collapse

link.title: Retro Achievement Mastery 217 - Final Fantasy 1 - Economic Collapse

entry.content:
So, I’ve beaten FF1 a few times now. Already mastered it, even. So what’s this, exactly? Well, this is a romhack of FF1 where it’s… FF1 again, but there’s no stores.

That’s it.

So basically, it’s FF1. But okay, so the way it works is this: the game is unbelievably rough in the first 2/5ths or so, until you get enough items from dungeons that the last 3/5ths are just Final Fantasy 1. But UNTIL then, no pure potions, no potions in general, if you’re inflicted with Stone you should just reload frankly.

Is this worth playing over FF1? No, obviously not. Is it even worth playing if you just wanna replay FF1? Uh, well… if you really like FF1? Which I do. I just kinda wanted an NES RPG to grind out while watching Youtube videos. Which this is even more that than FF1. So, hell yea. Got what I wanted.
